input.initialPrompt: Modernize this project to SDK-style format. Identify all legacy patterns and suggest how to simplify the project file.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize it.

Legacy patterns found:
1. `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and XML namespace - not needed in SDK-style
2. Explicit `<Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)...Microsoft.Common.props"` - replaced by SDK reference
3. `<Configuration>` and `<Platform>` conditionals - SDK defaults
4. `<ProjectGuid>` - not needed
5. `<FileAlignment>` - SDK default
6. `<AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>` - SDK default


7. `<Deterministic>` - SDK default (true)
8. Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` configurations - all SDK defaults
9. Explicit `<Reference>` to System assemblies - implicit with .NET Framework targeting
10. Explicit `<Compile Include=...>` items - SDK-style uses implicit globbing
11. `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` - replaced by auto-generated assembly attributes from project properties
12. `<Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />` - replaced by SDK
13. `<TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFramework>` - becomes `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` in SDK format

Now I'm converting this to an SDK-style project file. The AssemblyInfo.cs file can be deleted since the SDK automatically generates those attributes from the project properties, so I'll move that metadata directly into the .csproj instead.

    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>

    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolArgs.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.content:
Done. Here's a summary of the modernization:

**Legacy patterns removed (47 → 13 lines):**

| Legacy Pattern | Modernized |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion` + XML namespace | `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` attribute |
| Explicit `Import` of Common.props/CSharp.targets | Handled by SDK |
| `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| `Configuration`/`Platform` conditionals | SDK defaults |
| `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | SDK defaults |
| Debug/Release PropertyGroups (OutputPath, DefineConstants, etc.) | SDK defaults |
| Explicit `<Reference>` to System assemblies | Implicit with .NET Framework |
| Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | Implicit globbing |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Auto-generated from `<Version>`, `<Company>`, etc. |
| `OutputType Library` | SDK default |

The project file is now:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```
